name: Heavenly Wheelchair TRNSPRTN
districts_or_customers: Not identified; no public school district contracts found
recent_news: No public news found; no acquisition or PE ownership identified. Multiple similarly named NEMT operators exist in Temple Hills MD market.
fit_notes: Micro-operator (8 employees, ~$480K rev, 2025 record). Appears to be NEMT/wheelchair only with no confirmed school-bus or SPED district contracts; not a viable Summit tuck-in.

name: Dallas Wheels Chair Trnsprtn
districts_or_customers: Not identified; no public school district contracts found
recent_news: No public news, M&A, or PE ownership identified. Distinct from larger 'Dallas Wheelchair Transport' brand in DFW metro.
fit_notes: Micro NEMT operator (~$463K rev, 6 employees) in Plano TX. No evidence of school-bus or SPED district routing; below Summit's tuck-in size/relevance threshold.

name: Medtrust Ambulance
owner_or_parent: MedTrust Holdings (PE-backed; investors include Charleston Angel Partners; Lenna Macdonald CEO since 2021)
districts_or_customers: Hospital systems and healthcare facilities across SC, GA, and FL; Lowcountry and Grand Strand regions; 30,000+ patients/year
recent_news: 2023 Inc. 5000 honoree as MedTrust Holdings. Named new CEO Lenna Macdonald in June 2021 with founder Josh Watts moving to board. Fastest-growing ambulance company in SC.
fit_notes: NOT a Summit target: this is MedTrust Medical Transport, a fast-growing institutionally-backed ambulance/interfacility healthcare transport platform — wrong vertical (medical, not school bus/SPED) and already a sponsor-backed platform itself. Exclude.

name: Advance Wheelchair Transport
districts_or_customers: Western NC / Henderson County area; Medicaid NEMT; no confirmed school district contracts
recent_news: NPI registered January 2019. Operates as 'Advance Wheelchair Transportation and Taxi Services' per Facebook. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Independent micro NEMT (6 employees, ~$378K) — owner age 60s suggests possible succession path, but vehicle count and service mix (NEMT/taxi, not school bus/SPED routing) make this sub-scale for Summit tuck-in.

name: Safe Ride LLC
districts_or_customers: Manchester NH area; no confirmed school district / SPED contracts identified. Manchester SD specialized transport is run by Easterseals NH and Manchester Transit Authority.
recent_news: Registered as LLC 8/2/2021. C+ BBB rating (1 unanswered complaint). No M&A, growth, or sponsor activity identified.
fit_notes: Very small (~$347K rev, 5 employees), young (2021) NEMT operator with no confirmed school district contracts and a BBB complaint. Not a Summit tuck-in candidate — Easterseals NH and Safe Wheels are the relevant Manchester NH SPED platforms.
